texto: How the hell would she support herself and her mother if Agua Dulce’s new owner built one of those mega service stations right next door? Or worse yet, right on top of them.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Anna Jeffrey que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “How the hell would she support herself and her mother if Agua Dulce’s new owner built one of thos…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, kindlehighlight, right, com foco em reflexao.

texto: Instinct caused a woman who had once been so attractive to still attempt makeup and hairdos, Marisa guessed, but more often than not, the effort came off with Mama looking like a clown. Seeing it broke Marisa’s heart, but she didn’t interfere. Her mother didn’t know the difference and these days, it was rare for anyone but Marisa to see her. What little family they had seldom came and Mama’s friends in Agua Dulce, out of respect, were reluctant to gawk at her decline.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Anna Jeffrey: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Instinct caused a woman who had once been so attractive to still attempt makeup and hairdos, Mari…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, kindlehighlight, reflexao pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e reflexao.

texto: You must learn to read well, Marisa. As long as you're a good reader, you can learn anything, do anything.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Anna Jeffrey sobre temas ligados a sabedoria. A afirmação “You must learn to read well, Marisa. As long as you're a good reader, you can learn anything, do …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a sabedoria, do, learn, útil em momentos de learn e read.

texto: But Marisa already knew the answer and it was too late for recrimination. The chance of even a rational discussion of the problem was forever shut out of Mama’s brain. A brutal bastard was steadily sucking the intelligence and the very life from the mother who had once been witty, wise and loving. The scourge had a name Marisa had come to equate with hell: Alzheimer’s Disease.
explicacao: Citação de Anna Jeffrey sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “But Marisa already knew the answer and it was too late for recrimination.
